titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of TNA DL 25/88

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=854 status=live modified=2021-04-04 16:29:47.686203+00 | translation moa=855 status=live modified=2021-04-04 16:29:49.581153+00

p.cl: Omnibus hoc scriptum visurus uel audituris Helena filia Rogeri quondam dicti porter salutem in domino sempiternam

Noueritis me in maxima necessitate mea vendidisse et concessisse domino meo domino Roberto de Brus domino vallis Anan
die vnam grangiam cum acra super quam fundatur que quidem stat ex opposito contra domum quondam
Patricii Scheltun ad capud ville de Anand per quod itur ad furcas pro quadam summa pecunie quam dictus
dominus Robertus de Brus michi in maxima necessitate mea premanibus pacauit . de qua quidem pecunia me teneo
contentam et pacatam . Tenendum et habendum dicto domino . Roberto de Brus et heredibus suis uel suis assingnatis libere qui
ete pacifice et honorifice absque ulla calumpnia mei uel heredum meorum . Reddendo inde annuatim ipse uel here
des sui Luminari sancte Marie de Anand vnum denarium ad Natale domini pro omni seruitio et seculari demanda . Ego
vero et heredes mei dictam grangiam cum acra super quam fundatur contra quoscumque warantizabimus in perpetuum
et defendemus .
In cuius rei testimonium huic scripto sigillum meum apposui

hiis testibus . Domino Roberto de Heriz
Radulfo de camera constabulario et multis aliis .
To all who shall view or hear this writing Helen the daughter of the late Roger called Porter wishes eternal salvation in the Lord.

Know me in my great need to have sold and granted to my lord Robert de Brus the lord of Annandale one grange with the acre upon which it is built and which stands opposite the dwelling of a certain Patrick of Shelton at the head of the toun of Annan on the way to the gallows in return for which the said Robert de Brus has paid to me a certain amount of money as an advance in my great need and in respect of which money I remain satisfied and paid. For the said lord Robert de Brus and his heirs or his assigns to hold and to have freely, quitly, peaceably, and honourably, without any claim on me or my heirs. He or his heirs are annually to render for it one penny at Christmas for the lighting of St Mary’s at Annan in return for every service and secular demand. I and my heirs shall warrant and protect for ever against anyone the same grange with the acre upon which it is built.
In testimony of this matter I have applied my seal to this writing.
With these witnesses, the lord Robert de Heriz, Ralph de Camera the constable, and many others.
